meta.title: Automagic Night Light
meta.description: Build a night light that turns on automatically when it's dark.
meta.learning_goal: Understanding how sensors can control LED brightness based on ambient light.
meta.code_hint: Read the LDR's analog value and turn the LED on when the light level is below a threshold

meta.title: Automagic Night Light
meta.description: Build a night light that turns on automatically when it's dark.
meta.learning_goal: Understanding how sensors can control LED brightness based on ambient light.
meta.code_hint: Read the LDR's analog value and turn the LED on when the light level is below a threshold

walkthrough.instruction: Place the photoresistor (LDR) on the breadboard with one leg in row 12, column E and the other leg in row 9, column E.
walkthrough.tip: The photoresistor changes resistance based on light — brighter light means lower resistance!

walkthrough.instruction: Put the 10000Ω resistor on the breadboard with one leg in row 9, column D and the other leg in row 5, column D.
walkthrough.tip: The resistor limits current so the component doesn't burn out.

walkthrough.instruction: Put the red LED on the breadboard with the long leg (anode) in row 20, column E and the short leg (cathode) in row 19, column E.
walkthrough.tip: The long leg is positive — it's called the anode!

walkthrough.instruction: Put the 220Ω resistor on the breadboard with one leg in row 19, column D and the other leg in row 15, column D.
walkthrough.tip: The resistor limits current so the component doesn't burn out.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a red wire from the Arduino's 5V pin to the breadboard at row 12, column D.
walkthrough.tip: This wire carries the signal between Arduino and your component.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a black wire from the breadboard at row 5, column E to the Arduino's GND pin.
walkthrough.tip: Black wires carry ground — the return path for electricity.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a green wire from the breadboard at row 9, column A to Arduino pin A0.
walkthrough.tip: This wire carries the signal between Arduino and your component.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a green wire from Arduino pin D13 to the breadboard at row 20, column A.
walkthrough.tip: This wire carries the signal between Arduino and your component.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a black wire from the breadboard at row 15, column E to the Arduino's GND pin.
walkthrough.tip: Black wires carry ground — the return path for electricity.
